market_summary: The global art market showed resilience with a 4% growth in 2025 ($59.6B), led by a strong US rebound. While high-end sales thrive, the market faces cautious optimism due to macroeconomic uncertainties.
art_insights: Structural shifts continue as top-tier sales ($10M+) dominate, while mid-market segments adjust to rising operational costs. Collectors prioritize quality and provenance over speculation.
key_events: Global art sales reached $59.6 billion in 2025 (+4%)
US market remains dominant with 44% of global share
Shift from digital-first to in-person/hybrid transactions
